title: Co Loa Citadel
summary: A magnificent ancient construction technique featuring a unique spiral-shaped citadel, the most fortified in Southeast Asia at the time.

slides.text: To protect his capital, An Duong Vuong ordered the construction of a massive citadel. Strangely, parts of the wall collapsed as soon as they were built.
slides.imagePrompt: Ancient workers building a wall, but sections are collapsing, frustration and mystery, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: With the help of the Golden Turtle (Kim Quy God), An Duong Vuong defeated the evil spirits and completed the citadel in a very short time.
slides.imagePrompt: Golden Turtle appearing with a glowing light, helping the King, magical scene, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The citadel was built in a spiral shape, consisting of 3 fortified concentric rings. Because of this shape, it was named Co Loa (Spiral Citadel).
slides.imagePrompt: Aerial view of Co Loa citadel, spiral shape, wide moats, children book illustration, cartoon style, flat design

slides.text: Surrounding the citadel were deep moats connecting to the Hoang River, allowing the army to deploy its naval forces easily.
slides.imagePrompt: Deep moats with water, soldiers on boats, effective defense system, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Co Loa was not only a military fortress but also a wonderful architectural work reflecting the wisdom of our ancestors.
slides.imagePrompt: Close up of the massive stone and clay walls of Co Loa, impressive and grand, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: What special shape does Co Loa Citadel have?
Who helped An Duong Vuong build the Co Loa Citadel?
How many fortified rings does Co Loa Citadel have?
